title: Module Boundary And Dependency Budget
claimBoundary: Maintainability-only guardrail for the public local beta; no release-claim expansion.

domains.changeRule: Change orchestration calculations in the kernel before adapting scheduler or route execution code.
Route run, lease, binding, schema, and repository behavior through the work-store owner or port before adding new table-level callers.

domains.owner: Channel command, rendering, and provider contracts
domains.changeRule: Define provider-neutral commands, rendering, and capabilities first; concrete provider adapters only translate provider send/receive behavior.
Reason-coded allow, deny, preview, and human-required decisions belong in the shared security policy or authorization model before callers render them.
Remote execution, sandbox, lease, cleanup, and result-acceptance behavior belongs in runtime isolation contracts before scheduler/worker surfaces consume it.
Evidence exports, incident bundles, and release proof must use shared redaction and claim-safety helpers before docs or dashboards cite them.

domains.owner: Mission Control query and view model
domains.changeRule: Dashboard, MCP, and support surfaces consume typed Mission Control view models rather than reimplementing source-state policy.
CLI and daemon routes stay edge-focused: parse input, call owner modules, render output, and avoid becoming policy or storage owners.
Release checks must be scripted or have explicit skip semantics before release wording depends on them.
Support output must preserve redaction, source freshness, failure class, and safe-next-action contracts before changing operator-facing copy.

forbiddenImports.reason: Provider-neutral channel contracts must not depend on Telegram, WhatsApp, Discord, or future concrete adapters.
forbiddenImports.reviewCondition: A new provider-specific capability must be expressed in channels/capabilities or channels/renderer first, then translated by the adapter.

forbiddenImports.reason: Provider adapters should translate inbound/outbound provider behavior; scheduler, dashboard, MCP, and daemon routes own runtime execution.
forbiddenImports.reviewCondition: Route provider-neutral behavior through channel commands, channel sync, queue events, or a new narrow port.

forbiddenImports.reason: Work-store ports are storage-domain interfaces; they must not reach upward into scheduler, CLI, daemon, provider, dashboard, or MCP edges.
forbiddenImports.reviewCondition: Add a work-store operation to the port or owner module, then call it from the edge.

forbiddenImports.reason: The orchestration kernel is a pure calculation seam for retry/capacity timelines and must not directly own storage, HTTP, channel, or OpenCode side effects.
forbiddenImports.reviewCondition: Return an explicit plan from the kernel and execute it in scheduler or an adapter.

forbiddenImports.reason: Mission Control view-model policy should stay deterministic and adapter-free; data collection and rendering edges belong elsewhere.
forbiddenImports.reviewCondition: Add typed input data to the view model instead of importing a live adapter or storage edge.

forbiddenImports.reason: Channel session routing is the provider-neutral binding facade over durable bindings; it must not become a scheduler, daemon, dashboard, MCP, or provider-adapter owner.
forbiddenImports.reviewCondition: Add a channel-session method or work-store bindings-port operation first, then call it from command, dashboard, MCP, or provider edges.

forbiddenImports.reason: Dashboard rendering should consume Mission Control/read-model contracts and must not bypass them to mutate durable state, execute scheduler behavior, or depend on concrete provider adapters.
forbiddenImports.reviewCondition: Add a typed Mission Control input or read-model field first, then render it in the dashboard.

forbiddenImports.reason: MCP tools are local edge surfaces; they must authorize, call daemon endpoints, and format typed view-model data rather than importing mutable storage, scheduler, dashboard, or provider-adapter owners.
forbiddenImports.reviewCondition: Expose behavior through a daemon route, security policy, or Mission Control view model before wiring a new MCP tool.

forbiddenImports.reason: Evidence and redaction helpers must not embed provider-adapter or scheduler behavior because release proof consumers need stable, source-agnostic contracts.
forbiddenImports.reviewCondition: Record provider evidence through typed evidence inputs or incident-bundle sources, then redact through the shared helper.

forbiddenImports.reason: JOE-495 validation gate selector maps changed repo paths to local validation commands and warning-only performance budgets; it must not become a runtime dependency or reach mutable storage, scheduler execution, daemon/dashboard/MCP/channel/provider adapters, hosted execution, registry/package execution, CI/network behavior, or release artifact side effects.
forbiddenImports.reviewCondition: If selector recommendations need runtime or provider proof, route that proof to the owning runtime-proof issue first; keep JOE-495 limited to deterministic path classification, local command recommendations, full-verify escalation, and review-gate evidence.

forbiddenImports.reason: Security and authorization policy should classify provider targets through typed inputs, not concrete provider adapters or channel-command handlers.
forbiddenImports.reviewCondition: Add a reason-coded policy input or capability boundary entry before rendering provider-specific behavior.

ownerCategories.description: Scheduler, replay, quota, dispatch, and orchestration runtime behavior.
Work-store, durable state, repository, and persistence behavior.
Runtime isolation, remote execution, sandbox, and capability-grant behavior.
Provider-neutral channel commands plus concrete provider adapter edges.
Mission Control dashboard, MCP, and support read-model surfaces.
CLI and daemon route edges that execute or expose owner behavior.
Evidence export, redaction, incident, proof-run, and receipt safety contracts.
Release checks, validation gates, generated evidence reports, and proof taxonomy.
Support diagnostics, readiness, and operator support evidence.
Documentation, MkDocs navigation, public wording, and release-claim prose.
Validation selectors, review gates, release scripts, and CI/local gate orchestration.

allowedWhen: a manifest entry or generated report cannot express the proof yet
the module is deterministic and has no runtime side effects
a forbidden-import rule keeps it out of runtime, channel, scheduler, storage, daemon, dashboard, MCP, CI, network, and package-execution surfaces
the budget increase includes owner category, metric, previous/current values, rationale, and consolidation path
consolidateWhen: two or more static reports duplicate manifest metadata, blocked wording, redaction checks, or validation gates
a new evidence module only renders a standard static report that the M58 template can generate
runtime proof can be represented as a receipt consumed by the taxonomy instead of bespoke report code
failWhen: the change lacks budget rationale or owner classification
evidence or release code imports mutable runtime/storage/scheduler/channel/daemon surfaces without an explicit owner-approved forbidden-import exception
the wording expands release/product claims beyond the attached proof class

budgetIncreases.rationale: Adds deterministic runtime-proof taxonomy and redacted receipt validation so static and fixture evidence cannot satisfy stronger runtime/live/soak/provider/production claims.
budgetIncreases.consolidationPath: Use this taxonomy plus manifest entries to avoid future bespoke proof-class reports; migrate fixture/live/soak evidence to receipts before adding more modules.

budgetIncreases.rationale: One edge lets the taxonomy reuse shared redaction scanning; one edge lets the manifest registry enforce claim-strength requirements with the taxonomy.
budgetIncreases.consolidationPath: Keep future static evidence on the manifest/template path and attach runtime receipts instead of introducing new taxonomy imports.

budgetIncreases.rationale: M58-9 adds one deterministic final decision module that composes existing M58 evidence lanes, residual risks, M59 routing, module-boundary trend, and redaction checks without runtime behavior.
budgetIncreases.consolidationPath: Keep this as the final M58 decision record; future milestone decisions should prefer manifest/template reuse or consolidate static report scaffolding before adding bespoke modules.

budgetIncreases.rationale: M58-9 composes M58 scope, manifest, template, validation selector, runtime-proof taxonomy, redaction scanner, and release CLI outputs through release-ops-only imports.
budgetIncreases.consolidationPath: If final decision modules keep accumulating, consolidate shared decision composition helpers under the evidence report template or manifest registry.

budgetIncreases.rationale: M59-1 adds one deterministic runtime-kernel ownership and public-beta scope-gate module that maps owner surfaces, dogfood failures, dependency order, and claim boundaries without runtime behavior.
budgetIncreases.consolidationPath: Use this as the opening M59 planning artifact; future M59 runtime proof should attach receipts or reuse manifest/template infrastructure before adding bespoke static modules.

budgetIncreases.rationale: M59-1 imports the M58 final decision, shared redaction scanning, and the release CLI edge so scope-gate evidence is generated, redacted, and operator-visible through release-ops-only paths.
budgetIncreases.consolidationPath: If more M59 planning artifacts appear, consolidate static scope-gate composition into manifest/template helpers instead of adding new release-ops imports.

budgetIncreases.rationale: Extract work-store and environments type modules under file LOC budgets (audit P1-4) without expanding release claims.
budgetIncreases.consolidationPath: Continue splitting god façades into work-store/* and environments/* ports; lower maxModuleCount after orphan modules are deleted.

budgetIncreases.rationale: Type-module re-exports and imports from work-store/environments façades increase graph edges during LOC budget extraction.
budgetIncreases.consolidationPath: Collapse re-export edges by having consumers import work-store/types and environments/types directly where safe.

budgetIncreases.rationale: Extract work-presentation helpers and config schema/normalize leaves so work.ts and config.ts stay under soft LOC target 1500 without expanding release claims.
budgetIncreases.consolidationPath: Keep leaves pure; consumers continue to import façades (workRoutes/config) unless a second extraction needs direct leaf imports.

budgetIncreases.rationale: work.ts→work-presentation and config.ts→config-schema/config-normalize plus presentation helper imports increase graph edges during LOC façade split.
budgetIncreases.consolidationPath: Prefer keeping presentation helpers behind workRoutes; collapse duplicate façade re-export edges if leaves stabilize.

budgetIncreases.rationale: Extract scheduler-helpers, work-store/project-orchestration, mcp-tools-ops, and channel-commands-helpers so god façades approach soft LOC 1500 without expanding release claims.
budgetIncreases.consolidationPath: Keep leaves pure/orchestration-only; consumers continue to import façades (scheduler/work-store/mcp/channel-commands) unless a second extraction needs direct leaf imports.

budgetIncreases.rationale: Façade→leaf imports for scheduler, work-store orchestration, MCP ops tools, and channel-command helpers increase graph edges during LOC split.
budgetIncreases.consolidationPath: Prefer façade re-exports; collapse duplicate re-export edges if leaves stabilize.

directionalImportPilots.rationale: The validation gate selector may classify changed repo paths but must not import mutable runtime, storage, scheduler, channel, daemon, dashboard, MCP, CI, or package side effects.
